#### Arrhenius model for analyte stability

The model parameters calculated in **Tab.S2** are visualized in **Fig.S3** 
plotting the log transformed negative $k_{\mathit{eff}}(T)$ over the inverse 
temperature [1/K].

```{r FigS3, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="**Fig.S3** Arrhenius model plot", fig.height=4, fig.width=4}
x <- data.frame(
 "dummy_name" = c("4", "23", "60"),
 "1/K" = 1 / (c(4, 23, 60) + 273.15),
 "log(-k_eff)" = c(-6,-5,-4),
 "CI_upper" = c(-6,-5,-4)-c(0.2,0.1,0.3),
 "CI_lower" = c(-6,-5,-4)+c(0.2,0.1,0.3),
 "log(k)_calc" = 1:3,
 check.names = FALSE
)
eCerto:::prepFigS3(tab = x)
```

The axis at the top of **Fig.S3** indicates the corresponding temperature level 
for each data point in °C. The dashed red lines indicate the confidence interval 
As given in **Tab.S2**. Data points for T~i~ where k~eff~>0 are omitted in the 
plot, as the log transformation is not defined for non-negative values.
